audit_name: Aurexis Core V1 Substrate Candidate — Provenance Audit
audited_by: engineering repo review (not a formal legal opinion)
release: Aurexis Core Official Release 1 — V1 Substrate Candidate
scope: ACOR-1 release zip contents only (188 files)

findings.note: Authored by Vincent Anderson. No external imports except optional Pillow (see below).
Optional try/except import with stdlib fallback. Public API only. HPND license. No source redistribution.
Authored project tests. Uses pytest public API; pytest itself is not shipped.

repo_tree_flags.note: Personal ChatGPT conversation exports. Never inspected in audit. Flagged for privacy/public-repo hygiene.
Misnamed legacy local snapshot zip of project's own files. Not third-party.

third_party_dependencies_optionally_used.name: Pillow
third_party_dependencies_optionally_used.usage: public-API call with stdlib fallback in raster_law_bridge_v1.py

third_party_dependencies_optionally_used.name: pytest
third_party_dependencies_optionally_used.usage: optional host-side test runner; fallback run_pytest_surface.py ships in zip

remaining_uncertainty: This audit is an engineering-grade review, not a formal legal opinion.
ChatGPT-*.json personal exports were intentionally NOT opened or inspected.
Final public-redistribution legal determination is the owner's responsibility.
honest_verdict: ACOR-1 release zip contains no third-party source code and no HIGH-RISK / PROVENANCE-UNCLEAR findings. Clean-room rewrites were not needed. Repo-tree hygiene items are addressed via .gitignore.

re_verification.scope: Full re-scan of 66 V1 source modules + 2 fixture-support modules + pytest tests + standalone runners + run_pytest_surface.py

external_imports_found_in_release_zip.PIL: optional, public-API only, stdlib fallback, HPND license

re_verification.verdict: First-pass audit findings remain valid; ACOR-1 release zip still clean for public release under original framing.
